faults.name: Input capacitor open
faults.why: An open input capacitor blocks the changing input current, so the output stays near zero.
faults.verification_test: Power off and check capacitor continuity/path from Vin to the inverting node.

faults.name: Feedback resistor wrong value
faults.why: The differentiator output scale is set by Rf*C, so a wrong Rf changes spike amplitude.
faults.verification_test: Compare output spike amplitude with the expected Rf*C scale.

faults.name: Band-limit resistor missing
faults.why: A raw differentiator amplifies noise and can oscillate without practical limiting resistors.
faults.verification_test: Measure output noise with the input grounded.
faults.fix_recipe: Add the specified input/feedback limiting network.
